text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on breadth, but tech still owned.  
The tape split between a powerful semiconductor/AI bid and softer index-level follow-through, with the day’s strength concentrated in names tied to AI earnings and guidance while broader benchmarks were flatter to lower. Rotation stayed alive underneath the surface, but the clearest read was still selective accumulation in AI hardware rather than a clean all-in breakout.

INDICES — VIX was 15.8 and below its 20-day average of 17.1.  
Best-to-worst: ^SOX +5.87%, QQQ -0.26%, SPY -0.70%, DIA -1.13%, IWM -1.37%; SPY and QQQ were both below the 200-day MA if using the reported broad tape weakness as the signal, which would matter for CTA flows.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio was 1.1x and QQQ vol_ratio was not provided, while IWM was 1.1x and IJH was 1.0x.  
Volume was gaining attention in SPY, QQQ, IWM, XLF, and XLP, while DIA, IJR, XLK, XLI, and XLY were quieter. That mix says large-cap index selling was not high-conviction, but the move was being accumulated in financials and staples while tech breadth was less supported.

SECTORS — XLE Energy +1.29%, XLV Healthcare +0.79%, XLP Staples +0.40%, XLB Materials +0.21%, XLI Industrials +0.00%, XLRE Real Estate +0.00%, XLU Utilities -0.43%, XLY Discretionary -0.73%, XLK Technology -1.00%, XLF Financials -1.15%, XLC Comms -1.31%.  
Top 2 Industries ETFs were USO +2.62% and UNG +2.09%; bottom 2 were URA -5.67% and MSOS -6.14%.

ROTATION — Money went into energy and healthcare, while money came out of communications and financials.  
The strongest group was semiconductor-linked AI hardware via ^SOX and MRVL, while the weakest were comms and software-adjacent names via XLC and WCLD. AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol was 0.78 and QQQ PCR_vol was 1.64, so Nasdaq hedging demand was elevated while SPY looked closer to neutral.  
SPY skew was +3.4% and QQQ skew was +9.5%, both showing put premium and fear, while VIX at 15.8 sat below its 20-day average of 17.1.

CROSS-ASSET — USO +2.62% and UNG +2.09% outpaced equities, keeping the geopolitics-to-commodities transmission chain active.  
That energy bid contrasted with GLD -0.99% and SLV -2.62%, showing the market preferred growth risk over defensive metals despite the Iran backdrop.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed was 50.7, or neutral.  
Top movers were MRVL +3.73% in semis, USO +2.62% in energy, and UNG +2.09% in energy; best 20d momentum was ^SOX at +30.30%, worst was GLD at -2.49%.

WATCH — MRVL +3.73% needs follow-through after a 3.4x volume spike and a clean AI-semiconductor bid.  
MAGS -1.08% deserves a look because 4.5x volume in the mega-cap AI basket can signal distribution if strength stays narrow.  
IJS -1.22% matters because 9.1x volume on a style ETF often marks a regime check in small-cap participation.
